jokes.text: MY CRAP IS WAY BETTER THAN THIS CRAP
jokes.setup: Characters browsing a garage sale commenting on the items
jokes.punchline: Nick's confident assertion that his junk is superior to other people's junk
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Nick's delusional confidence about his possessions

jokes.text: WHY DO PEOPLE SAVE THEIR BOOKS, LIKE...READ A BOOK TWICE?
jokes.setup: Looking at books at garage sale
jokes.punchline: Nick's bewilderment at the concept of rereading books
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nick moment - his anti-intellectual worldview expressed with genuine confusion

jokes.text: THAT WOULD BRING THE ANDOPOLIS DRUM KIT TO AN EVEN 30 PIECES. 31 PIECES.
jokes.setup: Finding bongos at garage sale
jokes.punchline: The immediate correction from 30 to 31 pieces shows obsessive detail about drum equipment
jokes.explanation: Shows the characters' detailed knowledge of their gear obsessions

jokes.text: I WAS PLAYING AMERICAN BAND WHILE HE WAS WATCHING 60 MINUTES
jokes.setup: Explaining why his dad is mad
jokes.punchline: The oblivious timing - playing loud music during a quiet news show
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Nick's lack of awareness about appropriate timing

jokes.text: NICK...WHERE ARE YOUR DRUMS?
jokes.setup: Nick talking about drum equipment
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's obvious question that reveals the central problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man delivery that cuts through Nick's delusion

jokes.text: THOSE DRUMS WERE MAKING YOU GO DEAF. OTHERWISE, YOU WOULD HAVE HEARD ALL THE TIMES THAT I TRIED TO TELL YOU TO CLEAN UP YOUR ACT
jokes.setup: Nick complaining about his drums being sold
jokes.punchline: Dad's sarcastic logic about the drums preventing Nick from hearing warnings
jokes.explanation: Clever circular logic that justifies the drum removal

jokes.text: WITH A LITTLE MORE EFFORT, YOU COULD END UP LIVING IN A CARDBOARD BOX
jokes.setup: Nick claiming he's been trying
jokes.punchline: Dad's sarcastic encouragement toward homelessness
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that shows the father's frustration through exaggeration

jokes.text: MAYBE THE DRUMS ARE MY FUTURE! WHY DON'T YOU THINK ABOUT THAT?
jokes.setup: Dad saying Nick's only property is his future
jokes.punchline: Nick's delusional belief in his drumming career prospects
jokes.explanation: Classic Nick delusion delivered with genuine conviction

jokes.text: HOW MUCH DID YOU SPEND ON DRUMS AND HOW MUCH DID YOU SPEND ON DRUGS?
jokes.setup: Nick demanding money for his sold drums
jokes.punchline: Dad's implication that Nick's money went to drugs
jokes.explanation: Sharp parental comeback that cuts to the real issue

jokes.text: THERE'S SOMETHING WRONG WITH THESE CANS!
jokes.setup: Watching a movie
jokes.punchline: Sam quoting The Jerk movie
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that sets up the geeks' movie obsession

jokes.text: THEY GET REASSIGNED TO EUROPE TO WORK ON A TOP SECRET MISSION CONCERNING THE URBAN ASSAULT VEHICLE. THEN THE STUPID BORDER GUYS WOULDN'T LET THEM IN.
jokes.setup: Neal claiming no one remembers the second half of Stripes
jokes.punchline: Bill's incredibly detailed recall of the exact plot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill moment showing his encyclopedic movie knowledge

jokes.text: SAM, I JUST BROKE UP WITH TODD
jokes.setup: Cindy approaching Sam privately
jokes.punchline: The casual way she drops this major news
jokes.explanation: Sets up Sam's dream scenario with matter-of-fact delivery

jokes.text: DON'T BE! TODD'S A JERK. HE'S ONLY INTERESTED IN ONE THING. ALL THESE JOCKS ARE.
jokes.setup: Sam apologizing for the breakup
jokes.punchline: Cindy's dismissive attitude toward her ex
jokes.explanation: Standard teen romance setup but well-delivered

jokes.text: MY GOD! IT'S HAPPENING! YOU GUYS, WHERE'S MY CHAIR?
jokes.setup: Cindy just left after asking Sam to call
jokes.punchline: Sam's over-the-top celebration and desperate need to sit down
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sam moment showing his inexperience with girls

jokes.text: SHALLOTS? IT'S A POTATO. THEY'RE SHALLOTS, RIGHT?
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to fit in with the freaks
jokes.punchline: Her confusion over basic cooking ingredients
jokes.explanation: Shows Lindsay's fish-out-of-water status with the freaks

jokes.text: GRATEFUL DEAD. MUSIC SUCKS. CHICKS ARE HOT!
jokes.setup: Looking at Grateful Dead album
jokes.punchline: Daniel's brutally honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Daniel line - concise, honest, and perfectly captures his priorities

jokes.text: DOES ANYBODY WANNA GIVE ME A NECK RUB?
jokes.setup: Nick sleeping on Daniel's floor
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate request in front of everyone
jokes.explanation: Shows Nick's lack of social awareness and desperation

jokes.text: BETTER THAN SLEEPING IN MY MAVERICK
jokes.setup: Kim commenting on Daniel's infested carpet
jokes.punchline: Nick's even worse alternative living situation
jokes.explanation: Reveals Nick's desperate living situation with understated delivery

jokes.text: HE DOESN'T EVEN LET MY GRANDMA STAY OVER. HE'S GOT ISSUES.
jokes.setup: Ken explaining why Nick can't stay over
jokes.punchline: The extreme example of his dad's strictness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the father's unreasonable nature

jokes.text: YOU GOTTA REMEMBER TO FLUSH THE TOILET! MY MOM ALMOST HAD A HEART ATTACK THIS MORNING.
jokes.setup: Daniel saying Nick blew his chance to stay over
jokes.punchline: The embarrassing bathroom incident
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor that reveals Nick's gross habits

jokes.text: I HAD TO PLUNGE--
jokes.setup: Daniel continuing the toilet story
jokes.punchline: The implication gets worse before Nick cuts him off
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that emphasizes Nick's desperation

jokes.text: IT'S CALLED 3 IRRATIONAL DAYS PER MONTH
jokes.setup: Lindsay asking why there can't be a woman president
jokes.punchline: Harold's sexist reasoning about menstruation
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate sexism that shows Harold's character flaws

jokes.text: MEN GET PERIODS, TOO. IT HAS TO DO WITH YOUR BODY TIDES.
jokes.setup: Arguing with her dad about periods
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's pseudo-scientific comeback
jokes.explanation: Great Lindsay moment showing her rebellious but naive nature

jokes.text: MY BODY DOES NOT HAVE A TIDE
jokes.setup: Lindsay's body tides comment
jokes.punchline: Harold's flat denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to Lindsay's absurdity

jokes.text: MY BODY DOES NOT HAVE A TIDE.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's body tides comment
jokes.punchline: Harold's literal denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response. Harold's matter-of-fact denial of having tides is absurdly funny.

jokes.text: LET ME GUESS. [SNIFFS] MEAT?
jokes.setup: Nick complimenting the dinner smell
jokes.punchline: His obvious and awkward guess
jokes.explanation: Shows Nick trying too hard to be charming but being awkward

jokes.text: IT WOULD...ROCK
jokes.setup: Nick using 'rock' to describe dinner
jokes.punchline: Harold awkwardly trying to use Nick's slang
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Harold's discomfort with youth slang

jokes.text: I THINK MY SCHOOLWORK WAS INTERFERING WITH MY DRUMS
jokes.setup: Explaining his dad's reasoning about drums and school
jokes.punchline: Nick's completely backwards priority system
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nick line that encapsulates his character - delusional but delivered with conviction

jokes.text: Harold's mortified reaction to Nick's statement
jokes.setup: Nick's backwards priority statement
jokes.punchline: Harold's visible horror at this attitude
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy - Harold realizing what kind of kid he's invited into his home

jokes.text: IT'S LIKE YOU COOKED THIS IN HEAVEN...THEN BROUGHT IT BACK DOWN HERE TO YOUR TABLE
jokes.setup: Nick praising Mrs. Weir's cooking
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top, awkward metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nick moment showing his tendency toward overwrought compliments

jokes.text: THAT WAS NEAL. HE JUST SAID IT WAS ME
jokes.setup: Girl mentioning something about yesterday
jokes.punchline: Bill's immediate panic and deflection
jokes.explanation: Shows Bill's anxiety around girls and quick blame-shifting

jokes.text: NO, I LIKE LIKE SAM
jokes.setup: Bill saying he likes Sam too
jokes.punchline: Cindy clarifying she means romantic interest
jokes.explanation: Standard teen romance clarification but well-delivered

jokes.text: CINDY WANTED ME TO FIND OUT IF YOU LI--
jokes.setup: Sam asking what happened
jokes.punchline: Bill getting cut off by the teacher just before revealing the secret
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom timing with teacher interruption

jokes.text: THEN YOU OFFER TO GIVE A BACK RUB. YOU DO IT RIGHT... YOU'RE IN.
jokes.setup: Giving Nick advice about Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Daniel's sleazy dating strategy
jokes.explanation: Shows Daniel's crude approach to relationships

jokes.text: I WOULDN'T TURN HER AWAY
jokes.setup: Nick claiming he and Lindsay are just friends
jokes.punchline: His immediate contradiction about what he'd do if she approached him
jokes.explanation: Shows Nick's transparent feelings despite his denials

jokes.text: IF HE TRIES TO GIVE YOU A FOOT MASSAGE...RUN
jokes.setup: Warning Lindsay about Nick's moves
jokes.punchline: The foot massage as the ultimate warning sign
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the back rub warning

jokes.text: UH, HEY, CINDY, YOU WANT TO GO WITH ME TO MONA'S PARTY? WELL, I WAS KIND OF HOPING THAT SAM WAS GONNA ASK ME, BUT SINCE HE DOESN'T SEEM INTERESTED, OK, THOR.
jokes.setup: Neal doing impressions of what other guys might say
jokes.punchline: His deep voice impression ending with 'Thor'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Neal's theatrical nature

jokes.text: THEY'RE SMILING.
jokes.setup: Sam asking Cindy to the party
jokes.punchline: The geeks' basic observational commentary from afar
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective moment showing the geeks as outside observers analyzing basic social cues.

jokes.text: THEY ARE. BUT THAT'S ONE CULT I WOULDN'T MIND JOINING. BRING ON THE PODS.
jokes.setup: Neal saying the popular kids are a cult
jokes.punchline: Bill's willingness to join despite the criticism
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Bill's priorities changing

jokes.text: YOU'RE STARTING TO SOUND LIKE MY DAD, MR. WEIR
jokes.setup: Harold telling Nick to do homework
jokes.punchline: Nick's complaint, not realizing Harold agrees with his dad
jokes.explanation: Shows Nick's lack of awareness about Harold's intentions

jokes.text: WELL, THEN, YOUR DAD IS A SMART MAN
jokes.setup: Nick comparing Harold to his dad
jokes.punchline: Harold's immediate agreement with Nick's dad
jokes.explanation: Perfect Harold response showing his solidarity with Nick's father

jokes.text: THAT'S NEIL PEART. HE'S THE GREATEST DRUMMER ALIVE. WELL, NEIL PEART COULDN'T DRUM HIS WAY OUT OF A PAPER BAG.
jokes.setup: Nick defending his musical idol
jokes.punchline: Harold's dismissive assessment of Nick's hero
jokes.explanation: Good generational conflict over musical taste

jokes.text: NICK WAS SO INTO ME, HE MADE ME WANT TO MOVE TO A DIFFERENT COUNTRY
jokes.setup: Discussing Nick's intensity
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's extreme reaction to Nick's attention
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that captures the intensity of teenage romantic pressure

jokes.text: I DON'T THINK NICK'S IN LOVE WITH YOU. I THINK HE'S IN LOVE WITH MOM AND DAD.
jokes.setup: Lindsay complaining about Nick
jokes.punchline: Sam's astute observation about Nick's real motivation
jokes.explanation: Excellent insight that reframes Nick's behavior - sharp writing that cuts to the truth

jokes.text: FRENCH KISSING IS GROSS. I WOULDN'T FRENCH KISS IN A MILLION YEARS.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the make-out party
jokes.punchline: Sam's naive rejection of French kissing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Sam's inexperience and anxiety

jokes.text: GERMS. SPIT. MUCUS. OLD BITS OF FOOD. THAT'S JUST TO NAME A COUPLE. THERE'S A LOT OF THINGS IN THERE.
jokes.setup: Neal asking why French kissing is gross
jokes.punchline: Bill's increasingly disgusting catalog of mouth contents
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill germaphobia escalation. The clinical listing of disgusting things builds well.

jokes.text: WHAT IF I THROW UP IN HER MOUTH?
jokes.setup: Bill's anxieties about kissing
jokes.punchline: His most extreme and disgusting fear
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Bill's neuroses to their most extreme conclusion

jokes.text: I'M SAVING MY VIRGINITY FOR THE FUTURE MRS. CRISP
jokes.setup: Being invited to the make-out party
jokes.punchline: Gordon's pretentious reason for not going
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gordon's grandiose self-image

jokes.text: THEN YOU ADD OR SUBTRACT A SMALL PERCENTAGE OF TORQUE BASED ON THE POSITION OF YOUR TARGET
jokes.setup: Explaining his bottle spinning technique
jokes.punchline: Neal's overly scientific approach to spin the bottle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Neal moment showing his tendency to over-engineer everything

jokes.text: WE'LL LOOK LIKE THE SMOTHERS BROTHERS
jokes.setup: Both wearing turtlenecks to the party
jokes.punchline: Neal's reference to the comedy duo
jokes.explanation: Good period-appropriate reference showing Neal's cultural awareness

jokes.text: NICE DICKEY!
jokes.setup: Bill removing his turtleneck
jokes.punchline: Neal mocking what's underneath
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about 1980s fashion

jokes.text: THEY PUT HIM IN THAT ROOM WITH THE BLIND GUY AND THE INDIAN GUY
jokes.setup: Bill's Animal House reference about not fitting in
jokes.punchline: His detailed memory of the movie scene
jokes.explanation: Shows Bill's movie obsession and fear of social rejection

jokes.text: WHEN I DO, YOU JUST YELL AT ME. THAT'S BECAUSE I EXPECT MORE FROM YOU
jokes.setup: Lindsay questioning why Harold is nicer to Nick
jokes.punchline: Harold's honest explanation about different expectations
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing parental psychology

jokes.text: HAROLD, HOW OLD IS YOUR SON? 14. YOU CALL ME WHEN HE TURNS 16.
jokes.setup: Harold giving parenting advice
jokes.punchline: Nick's dad's warning about teenage years getting worse
jokes.explanation: Good parenting humor about the challenges ahead

jokes.text: THERE IS OBVIOUSLY SOMETHING WRONG WITH THAT BOTTLE
jokes.setup: Bottle keeps landing on Bill
jokes.punchline: Neal blaming the bottle instead of his technique
jokes.explanation: Shows Neal's inability to accept his plan isn't working

jokes.text: YOU SURE YOU DON'T WANNA ADOPT ME?
jokes.setup: Having to leave the Weir house
jokes.punchline: Nick's half-serious offer
jokes.explanation: Shows Nick's desperation and attachment to the family

jokes.text: YOU'RE A JERK. I COULDN'T BE LESS HAPPY TO BE IN HERE WITH YOU
jokes.setup: Bill stuck in closet with Vicki
jokes.punchline: Vicki's brutal honesty about her feelings
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor showing the reality of teenage social rejection

jokes.text: I WATCH MOVIES IN MY HEAD
jokes.setup: Vicki asking what Bill laughs about
jokes.punchline: Bill's explanation for his constant happiness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character revelation that explains Bill's personality perfectly - original and touching

jokes.text: YOU'RE MY DAUGHTER. AND...EVERY SECOND YOU'RE OUT OF THIS HOUSE, EVERY SECOND THAT I CAN'T SEE YOU OR KNOW WHAT YOU'RE DOING, IT'S...IT'S ABSOLUTE TORTURE FOR ME.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asking about different treatment
jokes.punchline: Harold's heartfelt confession about parental anxiety
jokes.explanation: Touching character moment that balances humor with genuine emotion

jokes.text: WHY NOT?
jokes.setup: Lindsay saying she can't stay inside all the time
jokes.punchline: Harold's hopeful question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Harold moment showing his protective instincts

jokes.text: BILL, YOUR 7 MINUTES WERE UP 5 MINUTES AGO. VICKI, YOUR NIGHTMARE'S OVER.
jokes.setup: Bill and Vicki in the closet too long
jokes.punchline: The host's description of it as Vicki's nightmare
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment that emphasizes Bill's social rejection
